scores.metric: full-build unmasked Levenshtein vs pristine code6cac_c2.o (clean pin-free + named-const body)

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is clean pure C (named-const addPrim form; zero rules, zero asm — also removes HEAD's bogus `ori %0,%0,0xFFFF :: r(base)` coercion). Not committable only because of a register-allocation tie (non-matching), not a cheat.

remaining_gap.summary: Register-allocation tie in a 27-insn no-arg GPU OT-link (addPrim). Target assigns the three constants to $a1(code)/$a2(mask_lo)/$a3(mask_hi), keeping $a0 free for the *(u32*)p temp; GCC packs code->$a0 and swaps the masks ($a2<->$a3), and reorders the two *ot reads in the final RMW pair. The constants are independent in a no-arg function so GCC has full allocation freedom and its ascending-preference tiebreaker differs from target's arg-register layout.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD uses 4 register-asm pins (code a1, mask_lo a2, mask_hi a3, p v1) + a phantom ori-asm. No pure-C lever lands the no-arg constants on a1/a2/a3 with a0-as-temp (6 measured).

next_hypotheses: decomp-permuter from the pin-free base (27 insns, few relocations -> isolated score MORE faithful here than the larger walls; reasonable odds).
greg -da: confirm the constants' pseudos and why a0 isn't skipped; a liveness trick that forces a0-as-temp-first might cascade the constants up to a1-a3.
If walls: canonical-asm review (the a1/a2/a3 arg-register layout in a no-arg fn is a hand-asm hallmark).

rejected_forms.form: temps / ordered / inline_const (named consts, various orders)
rejected_forms.reason: best clean floor; does not land constants on a1/a2/a3
